kata.title: Pascal's Triangle II
kata.workload: each rep computes the rowIndex-th Pascal row for a data-dependent rowIndex (30+acc%20, so 30..49, seeded by the running hash so get_row() can't be hoisted), BULK-FILLING one Vec[i64] of length rowIndex+1 with ones (Vec.filled, matching Rust's vec![1;n]) and updating it IN PLACE right-to-left (row[k]=row[k]+row[k-1]), folding every entry; K=440000 reps — the in-place index-assignment regime (a single small Vec per rep, dominated by the O(k^2) in-place updates)

env.note: Measured on a shared x86-64 Linux cloud container (karac from kara main, post B-2026-07-17-1), NOT the corpus's Apple M5 Pro. Canonical M5 numbers in the sibling results.json are left untouched. Absolute times/sizes/RSS NOT comparable across the two files; only within-file cross-language ratios are the signal, and THIS run was noisy (sigma up to ~10%) so read ratios, not absolute ms. Seq lane (fastest first): c 230ms ~ rust 230ms < rust_ovf 293ms < kara 355ms < go 445ms. FILL PARITY: both mirrors bulk-fill the initial ones — kara Vec.filled(n,1), Rust vec![1;n] — an earlier revision filled kara with a Vec.new()+push loop (a scalar fill vs Rust's tuned bulk-fill), which distorted the margin; fixed. BOUNDS-CHECK GAP FIXED: kara B-2026-07-17-1 (fix 6474a73) eliminated the per-iteration bounds check on the in-place descending update; the DP update loop is now at equal-safety-Rust PARITY (isolated in-place-update microbench 1.01x; update-loop asm byte-identical to Rust's). The residual ~1.21x vs rust_ovf is NOT the DP loop: it is the O(k) fill + per-rep alloc/free + container noise, with no single isolable codegen gap (Vec.filled already vectorizes; when Rust is switched to a matching push-fill loop, kara is 1.45x FASTER). The earlier-suspected `cmp $1` loop guard (B-2026-07-17-14) was INVALIDATED — a drift-immune A/B showed the guard costs 0% wall-clock (counter-dependency-bound loop, branch predictor hides it). The overflow check (jo) is present in both kara and rust_ovf and is NOT the gap. All mirrors agree on the sink; correctness verified across interp/JIT/AOT/auto-par and valgrind-clean + ASAN-gated. Re-run bench.sh on the M5 to fold comparable numbers into the corpus tables.
